author: Igor Chilingarian
description: Panoramic view of the Milky Way center from Las Campanas Observatory on July 4, 2017.

author: D. Nelson / TNG Collaboration
description: A protocluster forming in the IllustrisTNG50 hydrodynamical simulation. Large dark matter halos (orange) are connected by a bridge of tenuous dark matter (blue). High-speed gas motions (white) reveal ram-pressure and tidal stripping of orbiting satellite galaxies, as well as galactic-scale outflows driven by feedback.

author: D. Nelson / TNG Collaboration
description: Dark matter density of the entire TNG100-1 cosmological simulation box (orange/white), overlaid on the average number of shocks along the line of sight (blue).

author: Springel et al. / Virgo Consortium
description: Projected dark matter density of the 'Aquarius A' Milky Way-like halo, showing the smooth main halo (orange) threaded by thousands of dark-matter subhalos (blue).

author: Via Team
description: Synthetic HST/WFC3 image of a simulated ultra-faint dwarf galaxy (1,000 solar masses, 15 pc half-mass radius) at a distance of 120 kpc. The composite maps the F814W band to red, F606W to green, F475W to blue, and F275W to purple.

author: Evan Schneider
description: Gas density field in an outflow simulation driven by a few massive star clusters near the center of the disk, performed with the Cholla astrophysics code at a fixed resolution.

author: NASA, ESA, J. Hester and A. Loll (Arizona State University)
description: The Crab Nebula — the expanding remnant of the supernova of 1054 AD — imaged by the Hubble Space Telescope.

author: Via Team
description: CAD model of the entire Viaspec system, with the fiber positioner mounted on the back of the 6.5 m MMT mirror and the bench spectrograph on a cart parked on the dome floor.

author: Via Team
description: CAD model of the Viaspec focal plane system, fed by 600 robotic fibers positioned by vertically-oriented actuators.

author: Via Team
description: Optical ray-trace of the Viaspec bench spectrograph, from the fiber slit through the collimator, diffraction grating, and camera onto the detector.

author: Via Team
description: CAD model of Boombox, the low-resolution companion spectrograph to Viaspec.

author: Igor Chilingarian
description: Panoramic view of the Mount Hopkins summit in September 2015, with the MMT dome open. The Andromeda galaxy is visible on the top left of the dome.

author: Igor Chilingarian
description: Panoramic view of the Cerro Manqui peak in July 2013, with the Magellan/Baade telescope on the right, Magellan/Clay on the left, and the Galactic center directly above them.

author: Via Team
description: Footprint of the Via survey in Galactic coordinates, showing the stream perturbation, dwarf galaxy, cold gas, and transient follow-up target fields.
